pattern: Splitting 2n numbers into equal halves to minimize the sum difference is a meet-in-the-middle search: enumerate every subset sum of each half by chosen-count, then pair counts that add to n while binary-searching for the closest match to half the total.
hints: Brute-force subset enumeration over all 2n elements is too slow, but splitting the array into two halves of size n each and enumerating subsets of each half separately is fast enough.
For each half, group its subset sums by how many elements were chosen; a full partition takes i elements from one half and (n - i) from the other, for every i from 0 to n.

Precompute, for each half, the sorted list of subset sums for every possible chosen-count. For each count i and each sum 'a' from the first half, binary-search the second half's (n - i)-count sums for the value closest to total/2 - a, and track the smallest resulting |total - 2*(a+b)|.

edge_inputs.description: large positive and negative values that must be split apart

stress.description: random arrays up to the maximum n=15 constraint
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 15))
    nums = [rng.randint(-10000000, 10000000) for _ in range(2 * n)]
    return [nums]
